highlights.summary: AllNighters could not hold onto the lead against Pocket 9s on Wednesday at Raddin Park and fell 5-4 despite leading by three going into the bottom of the 5th. Robert (The Godfather) Barrows was the offensive highlight for AllNighters going 3 for 3 with three line shots to the right side, while Patrick Hosman, Brendan (Btro) Trohon, and Ryan Overberg each added two hits. A single by Overberg put AllNighters on the board in the top of the second and then the crew rallied in the 4th. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Unfortunately they left 11 men on base, hit into two double plays, and stranded the bases loaded in the top of the 7th. Barrows, Overberg, and Joe Zametjis each drove in one run. Pocket 9s finished things off with a walk-off single RBI in the bottom of the 7th. If the Allnighters look to repeat this year, they better get their bats a little warmer.

AllNighters defeated Franchise 9-5 on Monday. AllNighters jumped out to the lead in the bottom of the first inning after Brendan (Btro) Trohon singled scoring one run, Joe Zametjis homered scoring three runs, and Darren (Dermbo) McDermott singled scoring one run. A single by Brendan (Bmac) McCarthy, a single by Trohon, and a double by Jason Farrell helped AllNighters extend their early lead in the second. Mike (The OG) Russo earned the win for AllNighters. The starting pitcher gave up seven hits and five runs over seven innings striking out none and walking none. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Patrick Hosman, McCarthy, Eric (E) Moody, and Trohon each collected two hits. Zametjis led AllNighters with three runs batted in going 1-for-3 on the day.
